creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text:
had it happen again, this time running mplayer via OpenAL [for some reason, mplayer with the newest kernel started stuttering OSS audio, but openAL works ok until THIS BUG happened again)

initial symptoms of audio crashing sounded like rapidly repeating 1 buffer's worth of audio, a kind of buzzing noise.  Shutting off mplayer stopped the audio, but left the driver in a 'locked up' state.

following audio lock-up I captured some things:

relevant 'dmesg' output (not network related)
pcm2: chn_write(): pcm2:virtual:dsp2.vp0: play interrupt timeout, channel dead
Apr  6 12:43:40 slash pulseaudio[68850]: [(null)] pid.c: Daemon already running.
Apr  6 12:43:40 slash pulseaudio[68852]: [(null)] pid.c: Daemon already running.
pcm2: chn_write(): pcm2:virtual:dsp2.vp1: play interrupt timeout, channel dead
pcm2: chn_write(): pcm2:virtual:dsp2.vp0: play interrupt timeout, channel dead

(apparently OpenAL invokes pulseaudio and tried to re-start it?)

will attempt to repro with some kind of newer pulseaudio port...
